title: The Golden Goose
lines: Once there was a man with three sons.
The youngest son, named Simpleton, was considered foolish by everyone.
One day his father sent him into the forest to cut wood.
He met an old man who was hungry, and though everyone had told him to take no notice of beggars, Simpleton shared his food.
In return, the old man told him where to find the Golden Goose.
Simpleton found the goose, which had feathers of pure gold.
Everyone who touched the goose became stuck to it.
A long procession of people followed Simpleton as they became attached to the goose.
He came to a king who was having a sad day, and when the king saw the strange procession, he laughed so hard that he recovered his good humor.
The king offered Simpleton his daughter's hand in marriage as a reward.
Simpleton married the princess, and because of his good heart and the magical goose, he lived happily ever after.
